summary.intent: Fix 8 code review findings (2 P1, 6 P2) from a Codex review pass on input components in the ars-ui codebase.
summary.outcome: All 8 fixes implemented across PinInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, and PasswordInput components; 7 new tests added; all 1966 tests pass; snapshots updated; commit pushed.

repo: PinInput uses a hidden input for form submission; native `required` on the hidden input is ineffective for validation — `aria-required` must be set on each visible cell input instead.
The codebase uses `sync_controlled` (not just `set`) to properly handle controlled value paths in PinInput SetProps.
SearchInput has a CancelDebounce message that must be explicitly emitted; it was previously a no-op.
The project uses xci-fast as a pre-push gate with 10 checks; all must be green before pushing.
Snapshot tests capture attribute presence (e.g., Required boolean) and need to be accepted after intentional changes.

code.finding: SetProps must call sync_controlled (not just set) to keep controlled value in sync on resize; focused_index must be nulled out when it falls out of range after length shrink.
required attribute should be on cell inputs as aria-required, not on the hidden input, because hidden inputs are skipped by native browser validation.
CancelDebounce must emit cancel_effect; on_props_changed must emit CancelDebounce when the debounce prop changes to cancel any active timer.
Change handler must guard with is_finite() to reject NaN and Inf values; native required attr must be set alongside aria-required.
Native required attribute was missing from the input element; must be set alongside aria-required.

workflow: Poll script correctly detected review completion and wrote verdict, but the completion notification to the assistant was silently dropped — a known best-effort delivery limitation of the polling skill.
Snapshots should be verified for scope (only expected changes) before accepting, to catch unintended regressions.
Batching related fixes by component (then running a single full test pass) is more efficient than test-after-each-fix.

friction: Poll script notification delivery is best-effort and silently dropped the completion event, requiring the user to manually flag that reviews were ready.
Required attribute handling differs between visible and hidden inputs, requiring separate logic paths that are non-obvious.
open_items: Spec documentation was updated for PinInput only; SearchInput, NumberInput, and PasswordInput spec files may also need updates to document the required attribute and debounce cancellation behavior changes.
The polling/notification reliability issue was not fixed — it remains a known best-effort gap in the review workflow.
